summary: Frontend i18n testing complete for HumanisKind website. All 59 assertions passed across desktop and mobile viewports. Language switcher (desktop + mobile), browser default detection, localStorage persistence (key 'hik-lang'), navbar/footer/hero translations in EN/ES/PT, and full rendering across all 6 pages (/, /technology, /origins, /roadmap, /manifesto, /executive-summary) verified. No console errors, no '[object Object]' or undefined translation strings detected.
critical_code_review_comments: LanguageContext.jsx and Layout.jsx are clean and well-structured. LanguageSwitcher correctly handles outside-click close, persists lang to localStorage on change via useEffect, and falls back to English safely if a translation dictionary is missing (allTranslations[lang] || allTranslations.en).
Minor: PT flag uses 🇧🇷 (Brazilian flag) while label says 'Português' — intentional choice (Brazilian Portuguese) but worth confirming with stakeholders if European Portuguese users are a target.
All language buttons have proper data-testid attributes (lang-btn-{code}, lang-btn-mobile-{code}, language-switcher-button, language-switcher-menu, language-switcher-mobile).
context_for_next_testing_agent: i18n is fully working with EN/ES/PT. All testids in place. If new pages or sections are added, ensure they consume t.* keys and add equivalent entries in en.js/es.js/pt.js. Tested viewports: 1920x800 (desktop) and 390x844 (mobile). localStorage key is 'hik-lang'.
